is_bipartite:
FUNCTION IsBipartite_Directed(edge_list):
    adj ← empty map from node to set of nodes
    nodes ← empty set

    FOR EACH (u, v) IN edge_list:
        ADD u TO nodes
        ADD v TO nodes

        IF u NOT IN adj:
            adj[u] ← empty set
        IF v NOT IN adj:
            adj[v] ← empty set

        ADD v TO adj[u]
        ADD u TO adj[v]      # treat as undirected

    color ← empty map from node to integer

    FOR EACH node IN nodes:
        IF node NOT IN color:
            color[node] ← 0
            queue ← empty queue
            ENQUEUE node INTO queue

            WHILE queue IS NOT empty:
                x ← DEQUEUE queue

                FOR EACH y IN adj[x]:
                    IF y NOT IN color:
                        color[y] ← 1 − color[x]
                        ENQUEUE y INTO queue
                    ELSE IF color[y] = color[x]:
                        RETURN FALSE

    RETURN TRUE
clustering:
FUNCTION ClusteringCoefficient_Directed(edge_list, node):
    neighbors ← empty set
    edges ← empty set

    FOR EACH (u, v) IN edge_list:
        ADD (u, v) TO edges

        IF u = node AND v ≠ node:
            ADD v TO neighbors
        IF v = node AND u ≠ node:
            ADD u TO neighbors

    k ← SIZE(neighbors)
    IF k < 2:
        RETURN 0

    count ← 0
    FOR EACH a IN neighbors:
        FOR EACH b IN neighbors:
            IF a ≠ b AND (a, b) IN edges:
                count ← count + 1

    RETURN count / (k × (k − 1))
topological_sort:
Algorithm TopologicalSort_Kahn(G = (V, E))
Input:  Directed graph G
Output: List L containing vertices in topological order, or error if cycle

1  indeg[u] ← number of incoming edges for each u ∈ V
2  Q ← queue of all vertices u with indeg[u] = 0
3  L ← empty list

4  while Q not empty do
5      u ← dequeue(Q)
6      append u to L
7      for each v in Adj[u] do
8          indeg[v] ← indeg[v] − 1
9          if indeg[v] = 0 then
10             enqueue(Q, v)

11 if |L| < |V| then
12     report "Cycle detected" and stop
13 else
14     return L
hamiltonian_path:
Algorithm HamiltonianPath_Dp(G = (V, E))
Input:  n = |V|, label vertices as {0,…,n−1}, adjacency Adj
Output: A Hamiltonian path P if it exists; otherwise ⊥

1  DP[S][v] ← false for all subsets S ⊆ {0,…,n−1}, v ∈ S
2  parent[S][v] ← NIL
3  for v in 0..n−1 do DP[{v}][v] ← true

4  for each subset S with |S| ≥ 2 do
5      for each v ∈ S do
6          for each u ∈ S \ {v} do
7              if DP[S \ {v}][u] and (u,v) ∈ E then
8                  DP[S][v] ← true;  parent[S][v] ← u;  break

9  S* ← {0,…,n−1}
10 for v in 0..n−1 do
11     if DP[S*][v] then                      ▷ found an endpoint
12         P ← empty list
13         cur ← v;  T ← S*
14         while cur ≠ NIL do
15             prepend cur to P
16             prev ← parent[T][cur]
17             T ← T \ {cur};  cur ← prev
18         return P
19 return ⊥
strongly_connected_components:
Algorithm StronglyConnectedComponents(G)
Input:  Directed graph G = (V, E)
Output: A generator (sequence) of strongly connected components (SCCs), 
        each SCC is a set of vertices

1  preorder ← empty map            ▷ preorder number of each node
2  lowlink ← empty map             ▷ smallest preorder reachable
3  scc_found ← empty set           ▷ all nodes already assigned to an SCC
4  scc_queue ← empty stack         ▷ helper stack
5  i ← 0                           ▷ preorder counter
6  neighbors[v] ← iterator of G[v] for each v ∈ V

7  for each source ∈ V do
8      if source ∉ scc_found then
9          queue ← [source]        ▷ explicit DFS stack
10         while queue ≠ ∅ do
11             v ← top(queue)
12             if v ∉ preorder then
13                 i ← i + 1
14                 preorder[v] ← i
15
16             done ← true
17             for each w from neighbors[v] do
18                 if w ∉ preorder then
19                     push(queue, w)
20                     done ← false
21                     break
22
23             if done = true then
24                 lowlink[v] ← preorder[v]
25                 for each w ∈ G[v] do
26                     if w ∉ scc_found then
27                         if preorder[w] > preorder[v] then
28                             lowlink[v] ← min(lowlink[v], lowlink[w])
29                         else
30                             lowlink[v] ← min(lowlink[v], preorder[w])
31
32                 pop(queue)
33                 if lowlink[v] = preorder[v] then
34                     scc ← {v}
35                     while scc_queue not empty 
                           and preorder[top(scc_queue)] > preorder[v] do
36                         k ← pop(scc_queue)
37                         add k to scc
38                     add all vertices in scc to scc_found
39                     yield scc
40                 else
41                     push(scc_queue, v)
is_regular:
FUNCTION IsRegularDigraph(edge_list):
    in_degree ← empty map from node to integer
    out_degree ← empty map from node to integer

    FOR EACH (u, v) IN edge_list:
        IF u NOT IN out_degree:
            out_degree[u] ← 0
        IF v NOT IN out_degree:
            out_degree[v] ← 0
        IF u NOT IN in_degree:
            in_degree[u] ← 0
        IF v NOT IN in_degree:
            in_degree[v] ← 0

        out_degree[u] ← out_degree[u] + 1
        in_degree[v] ← in_degree[v] + 1

    nodes ← UNION of keys in in_degree and out_degree

    IF nodes IS empty:
        RETURN TRUE

    in_values ← SET of all in_degree[node] for node IN nodes
    out_values ← SET of all out_degree[node] for node IN nodes

    IF SIZE(in_values) = 1 AND SIZE(out_values) = 1:
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE:
        RETURN FALSE
has_eulerian_path:
Algorithm EulerianProperties(G, source=None)
Input:  Graph G (directed or undirected), optional start node source
Output: "circuit" if Eulerian circuit exists,
        "path" if Eulerian path exists but no circuit,
        None otherwise

1  if G is directed then
2      # ----- Eulerian Circuit -----
3      if (for all v: indeg(v) = outdeg(v)) 
             and (G is strongly connected) then
4          return "circuit"

5      # ----- Eulerian Path -----
6      if source ≠ None and (outdeg(source) − indeg(source) ≠ 1) then
7          return None
8
9      unbalanced_in  ← 0
10     unbalanced_out ← 0
11     for each v in V do
12         if indeg(v) − outdeg(v) = 1 then
13             unbalanced_in  ← unbalanced_in + 1
14         else if outdeg(v) − indeg(v) = 1 then
15             unbalanced_out ← unbalanced_out + 1
16         else if indeg(v) ≠ outdeg(v) then
17             return None
18
19     if unbalanced_in ≤ 1 and unbalanced_out ≤ 1 
             and G is weakly connected then
20         return "path"
21
22  else   # ----- Undirected graph -----
23      if (all degrees are even) and (G is connected) then
24          return "circuit"
25
26      if source ≠ None and degree(source) mod 2 ≠ 1 then
27          return None
28
29      if (exactly 2 vertices have odd degree) and (G is connected) then
30          return "path"
31
32  return None
diameter:
FUNCTION Diameter_Directed(edge_list):
    adj ← empty map from node to set of nodes
    nodes ← empty set

    FOR EACH edge IN edge_list:
        IF SIZE(edge) < 2:
            RETURN NONE

        u ← edge[0]
        v ← edge[1]

        ADD u TO nodes
        ADD v TO nodes

        IF u NOT IN adj:
            adj[u] ← empty set
        ADD v TO adj[u]

        IF v NOT IN adj:
            adj[v] ← empty set

    IF nodes IS empty:
        RETURN 0

    FUNCTION BFS_MaxDistance(start):
        dist ← empty map
        queue ← empty queue

        dist[start] ← 0
        ENQUEUE start INTO queue

        WHILE queue IS NOT empty:
            x ← DEQUEUE queue
            FOR EACH y IN adj[x]:
                IF y NOT IN dist:
                    dist[y] ← dist[x] + 1
                    ENQUEUE y INTO queue

        RETURN maximum value in dist

    diameter ← 0

    FOR EACH node IN nodes:
        ecc ← BFS_MaxDistance(node)
        IF ecc > diameter:
            diameter ← ecc

    RETURN diameter
scc_and_diameter_nested:
FUNCTION SCC_UNDIRECTED_DIAMETERS_DESC(edges):

    # Build directed adjacency and reverse adjacency
    CREATE empty map ADJ
    CREATE empty map RADJ
    CREATE empty set NODES

    FOR each directed edge (u, v) in edges:
        ADD u to NODES
        ADD v to NODES
        ADD v to ADJ[u]
        ENSURE v exists in ADJ
        ADD u to RADJ[v]
        ENSURE u exists in RADJ

    IF NODES is empty:
        RETURN empty list

    # First pass of Kosaraju: compute finishing order
    CREATE empty set VISITED
    CREATE empty list ORDER

    FUNCTION DFS1(start):
        CREATE stack with (start, next_neighbor_index = 0)
        ADD start to VISITED

        WHILE stack not empty:
            (x, i) ← top of stack
            IF i < SIZE(ADJ[x]):
                y ← ADJ[x][i]
                INCREMENT next_neighbor_index of top of stack
                IF y not in VISITED:
                    ADD y to VISITED
                    PUSH (y, 0) onto stack
            ELSE:
                POP stack
                APPEND x to ORDER

    FOR each node v in NODES:
        IF v not in VISITED:
            DFS1(v)

    # Second pass of Kosaraju: extract SCCs
    CLEAR VISITED
    CREATE empty list SCCS

    FUNCTION DFS2(start):
        CREATE empty list COMPONENT
        CREATE stack with start
        ADD start to VISITED

        WHILE stack not empty:
            x ← POP stack
            ADD x to COMPONENT
            FOR each y in RADJ[x]:
                IF y not in VISITED:
                    ADD y to VISITED
                    PUSH y onto stack

        RETURN COMPONENT

    FOR each node v in ORDER in reverse order:
        IF v not in VISITED:
            ADD DFS2(v) to SCCS

    # Build undirected adjacency for diameter computation
    CREATE empty map UNDIRECTED
    FOR each node v in NODES:
        UNDIRECTED[v] ← empty set

    FOR each edge (u, v) in edges:
        ADD v to UNDIRECTED[u]
        ADD u to UNDIRECTED[v]

    # Compute diameter of each SCC
    FUNCTION BFS_ECCENTRICITY(source, ALLOWED):
        CREATE empty map DIST
        CREATE empty queue Q
        DIST[source] ← 0
        ENQUEUE source into Q

        WHILE Q not empty:
            x ← DEQUEUE Q
            FOR each y in UNDIRECTED[x]:
                IF y in ALLOWED AND y not in DIST:
                    DIST[y] ← DIST[x] + 1
                    ENQUEUE y into Q

        RETURN maximum value in DIST

    CREATE empty list DIAMETERS

    FOR each COMPONENT in SCCS:
        IF SIZE(COMPONENT) ≤ 1:
            ADD 0 to DIAMETERS
            CONTINUE

        ALLOWED ← set(COMPONENT)
        DIAM ← 0
        FOR each v in COMPONENT:
            ECC ← BFS_ECCENTRICITY(v, ALLOWED)
            IF ECC > DIAM:
                DIAM ← ECC

        ADD DIAM to DIAMETERS

    SORT DIAMETERS in descending order
    RETURN DIAMETERS
has_node:
FUNCTION HasNode(edge_list, node):
    FOR EACH edge IN edge_list:
        u ← edge[0]
        v ← edge[1]

        IF u = node OR v = node:
            RETURN TRUE

    RETURN FALSE
has_edge:
FUNCTION HasEdge_Undirected(edge_list, node_pair):
    FOR EACH edge IN edge_list:
        a ← edge[0]
        b ← edge[1]

        IF (a = node_pair[0] AND b = node_pair[1]):
            RETURN TRUE

    RETURN FALSE
find_cycle:
FUNCTION IsCyclic_Directed(edge_list):
    adj ← empty adjacency list
    FOR EACH (u, v) IN edge_list:
        ADD v TO adj[u]

    visited ← empty set
    stack ← empty set

    FUNCTION DFS(u):
        ADD u TO visited
        ADD u TO stack

        FOR EACH v IN adj[u]:
            IF v NOT IN visited:
                IF DFS(v) = TRUE:
                    RETURN TRUE
            ELSE IF v IN stack:
                RETURN TRUE

        REMOVE u FROM stack
        RETURN FALSE

    FOR EACH node IN adj:
        IF node NOT IN visited:
            IF DFS(node) = TRUE:
                RETURN TRUE

    RETURN FALSE
number_of_edges:
FUNCTION CountEdges(edge_list):
    count ← 0

    FOR EACH edge IN edge_list:
        IF LENGTH(edge) ≥ 2:
            count ← count + 1

    RETURN count
number_of_nodes:
FUNCTION CountNodes(edge_list):
    nodes ← empty set

    FOR EACH edge IN edge_list:
        IF LENGTH(edge) ≥ 2:
            ADD edge[0] TO nodes
            ADD edge[1] TO nodes

    RETURN SIZE(nodes)
has_path:
FUNCTION HasPath_Directed(edge_list, s, t):
    IF s = t:
        RETURN TRUE

    adj ← empty adjacency list
    FOR EACH (u, v) IN edge_list:
        ADD v TO adj[u]

    visited ← {s}
    queue ← [s]

    WHILE queue IS NOT empty:
        u ← DEQUEUE(queue)
        FOR EACH v IN adj[u]:
            IF v = t:
                RETURN TRUE
            IF v NOT IN visited:
                ADD v TO visited
                ENQUEUE(queue, v)

    RETURN FALSE
degree:
FUNCTION Degree(edge_list, node):
    degree ← 0

    FOR EACH (u, v) IN edge_list:
        IF u = node OR v = node:
            degree ← degree + 1

    RETURN degree
scc_and_eulerian_feasibility:
FUNCTION STRUCTURAL_COMPLEXITY_SCORE(edges):

    # Build graph structures
    CREATE empty map ADJ          # directed adjacency
    CREATE empty map RADJ         # reverse adjacency
    CREATE empty map UNDIRECTED   # undirected adjacency
    CREATE empty map INDEG
    CREATE empty map OUTDEG
    CREATE empty set NODES

    FOR each directed edge (u, v) in edges:
        ADD u to NODES
        ADD v to NODES

        ADD v to ADJ[u]
        ENSURE v exists in ADJ
        ADD u to RADJ[v]
        ENSURE u exists in RADJ

        ADD v to UNDIRECTED[u]
        ADD u to UNDIRECTED[v]

        OUTDEG[u] ← OUTDEG[u] + 1
        INDEG[v] ← INDEG[v] + 1
        ENSURE OUTDEG[v] exists
        ENSURE INDEG[u] exists

    IF NODES is empty:
        RETURN 0

    # ---------- Compute SCCs using Kosaraju ----------
    CREATE empty set VISITED
    CREATE empty list ORDER

    FUNCTION DFS1(start):
        CREATE stack with (start, next_index = 0)
        ADD start to VISITED
        WHILE stack not empty:
            (x, i) ← top of stack
            IF i < SIZE(ADJ[x]):
                y ← ADJ[x][i]
                INCREMENT next_index of top of stack
                IF y not in VISITED:
                    ADD y to VISITED
                    PUSH (y, 0) onto stack
            ELSE:
                POP stack
                APPEND x to ORDER

    FOR each v in NODES:
        IF v not in VISITED:
            DFS1(v)

    CLEAR VISITED
    CREATE empty list SCCS

    FUNCTION DFS2(start):
        CREATE empty list COMPONENT
        CREATE stack with start
        ADD start to VISITED
        WHILE stack not empty:
            x ← POP stack
            ADD x to COMPONENT
            FOR each y in RADJ[x]:
                IF y not in VISITED:
                    ADD y to VISITED
                    PUSH y onto stack
        RETURN COMPONENT

    FOR each v in ORDER in reverse order:
        IF v not in VISITED:
            ADD DFS2(v) to SCCS

    NUM_SCC ← SIZE(SCCS)
    LARGEST_SCC_SIZE ← maximum SIZE(C) over all C in SCCS

    # ---------- Check existence of directed Eulerian trail ----------
    ACTIVE ← all v in NODES such that INDEG[v] + OUTDEG[v] > 0

    FUNCTION ACTIVE_UNDIRECTED_CONNECTED():
        IF ACTIVE is empty:
            RETURN true
        start ← first element of ACTIVE
        CREATE queue Q
        CREATE set SEEN containing start
        ENQUEUE start into Q
        WHILE Q not empty:
            x ← DEQUEUE Q
            FOR each y in UNDIRECTED[x]:
                IF (INDEG[y] + OUTDEG[y] > 0) AND y not in SEEN:
                    ADD y to SEEN
                    ENQUEUE y into Q
        RETURN SIZE(SEEN) == SIZE(ACTIVE)

    FUNCTION HAS_DIRECTED_EULER_TRAIL():
        IF NOT ACTIVE_UNDIRECTED_CONNECTED():
            RETURN false

        START_CANDIDATES ← 0
        END_CANDIDATES ← 0

        FOR each v in ACTIVE:
            o ← OUTDEG[v]
            i ← INDEG[v]
            IF o == i:
                CONTINUE
            ELSE IF o == i + 1:
                START_CANDIDATES ← START_CANDIDATES + 1
            ELSE IF i == o + 1:
                END_CANDIDATES ← END_CANDIDATES + 1
            ELSE:
                RETURN false

        RETURN (START_CANDIDATES == 0 AND END_CANDIDATES == 0) OR
               (START_CANDIDATES == 1 AND END_CANDIDATES == 1)

    # ---------- Structural complexity score ----------
    IF HAS_DIRECTED_EULER_TRAIL():
        RETURN LARGEST_SCC_SIZE
    ELSE:
        RETURN NUM_SCC
